words.definitionEn: according to what you have heard or read; according to the way something appears
words.exampleEn: Apparently they are getting divorced soon.

words.definitionEn: connected with industry
words.exampleEn: an industrial dispute

words.definitionEn: agreed or approved of by most people in a society
words.exampleEn: Children must learn socially acceptable behaviour.

words.definitionEn: a deeply felt request for money, help or information that is needed immediately, especially one made by a charity or by the police
words.exampleEn: appeal for something, to launch a TV appeal for donations to the charity

words.definitionEn: that you cannot avoid or prevent
words.exampleEn: It was an inevitable consequence of the decision.

words.definitionEn: as is certain to happen
words.exampleEn: Inevitably, the press exaggerated the story.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of causing or getting a disease
words.exampleEn: to cause/prevent infection

words.definitionEn: to reach an opinion or decide that something is true on the basis of information that is available
words.exampleEn: infer something (from something), Much of the meaning must be inferred from the context.

words.definitionEn: a general rise in the prices of services and goods in a particular country, resulting in a fall in the value of money; the rate at which this happens
words.exampleEn: the fight against rising inflation

words.definitionEn: to make a deeply felt request, especially for something that is needed immediately
words.exampleEn: I am appealing (= asking for money) on behalf of the famine victims.

words.definitionEn: information
words.exampleEn: For more info, click here.

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody about something, especially in an official way
words.exampleEn: inform somebody, The government took this decision without consulting Parliament or informing the public.

words.definitionEn: a person or an animal that lives in a particular place
words.exampleEn: the oldest inhabitant of the village

words.definitionEn: to receive money, property, etc. from somebody when they die
words.exampleEn: inherit something from somebody, She inherited a fortune from her father.

words.definitionEn: happening at the beginning; first
words.exampleEn: There is an initial payment of £60 followed by ten instalments of £25.

words.definitionEn: at the beginning
words.exampleEn: Initially, the system worked well.

words.definitionEn: a new plan for dealing with a particular problem or for achieving a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: a United Nations peace initiative

words.definitionEn: coloured liquid for writing, drawing and printing
words.exampleEn: in ink, written in ink

words.definitionEn: inside; towards or close to the centre of a place
words.exampleEn: an inner courtyard

words.definitionEn: the introduction of new things, ideas or ways of doing something
words.exampleEn: an age of technological innovation

words.definitionEn: introducing or using new ideas, ways of doing something, etc.
words.exampleEn: There will be a prize for the most innovative design.

words.definitionEn: time, knowledge, ideas, etc. that you put into work, a project, etc. in order to make it succeed; the act of putting something in
words.exampleEn: I'd appreciate your input on this.

words.definitionEn: an official process to find out the cause of something or to find out information about something
words.exampleEn: a murder inquiry

words.definitionEn: to put something into something else or between two things
words.exampleEn: insert something (in/into something), Insert coins into the slot and press for a ticket.

words.definitionEn: an understanding of what something is like
words.exampleEn: There are many valuable insights in her book.

words.definitionEn: to demand that something happen or that somebody agree to do something
words.exampleEn: I didn't really want to go but he insisted.

words.definitionEn: an officer of middle rank in the police force
words.exampleEn: Inspector Maggie Forbes

words.definitionEn: to give somebody the desire, confidence or enthusiasm to do something well
words.exampleEn: inspire somebody, The actors' enthusiasm inspired the kids.

words.definitionEn: to fix equipment or furniture into position so that it can be used
words.exampleEn: They're planning to install a new drainage system.

words.definitionEn: the act of fixing equipment or furniture in position so that it can be used
words.exampleEn: installation costs

words.definitionEn: a particular example or case of something
words.exampleEn: instance of somebody/something, The report highlights a number of instances of injustice.

words.definitionEn: happening immediately
words.exampleEn: This account gives you instant access to your money.

words.definitionEn: immediately
words.exampleEn: Her voice is instantly recognizable.

words.definitionEn: an organization that has a particular purpose, especially one that is connected with education or a particular profession; the building used by this organization
words.exampleEn: The report was compiled by Germany's five leading economic research institutes.

words.definitionEn: a large important organization that has a particular purpose, for example a university or bank
words.exampleEn: The deal is backed by one of the country's largest financial institutions.

words.definitionEn: an arrangement with a company in which you pay them regular amounts of money and they agree to pay the costs, for example, if you die or are ill, or if you lose or damage something
words.exampleEn: health/medical insurance

words.definitionEn: to combine two or more things so that they work together; to combine with something else in this way
words.exampleEn: integrate into/with something, These programs will integrate with your existing software.

words.definitionEn: connected with or using a person’s ability to think in a logical way and understand things
words.exampleEn: Gifted children typically show great intellectual curiosity and a wide range of interests.

words.definitionEn: that you are trying to achieve or reach
words.exampleEn: the intended purpose

words.definitionEn: very great; very strong
words.exampleEn: We were all suffering in the intense heat.

words.definitionEn: a person who makes a formal request for something (= applies for it), especially for a job, a place at a college or university, etc.
words.exampleEn: There were over 500 applicants for the job.

words.definitionEn: to communicate with somebody, especially while you work, play or spend time with them
words.exampleEn: Teachers have a limited amount of time to interact with each child.

words.definitionEn: the act of communicating with somebody, especially while you work, play or spend time with them
words.exampleEn: the interaction between performers and their audience

words.definitionEn: connected with the inside of something
words.exampleEn: the internal structure of a building

words.definitionEn: to explain the meaning of something
words.exampleEn: The students were asked to interpret the poem.

words.definitionEn: the particular way in which something is understood or explained
words.exampleEn: Her evidence suggests a different interpretation of the events.

words.definitionEn: to say or do something that makes somebody stop what they are saying or doing
words.exampleEn: Sorry to interrupt, but there's someone to see you.

words.definitionEn: a period of time between two events
words.exampleEn: The interval between major earthquakes might be 200 years.

words.definitionEn: to enter a country, town, etc. using military force in order to take control of it
words.exampleEn: Troops invaded on August 9th that year.

words.definitionEn: the act of an army entering another country by force in order to take control of it
words.exampleEn: the German invasion of Poland in 1939

words.definitionEn: an official examination of the facts about a situation, crime, etc.
words.exampleEn: a criminal/murder/police investigation

words.definitionEn: the act of investing money in something
words.exampleEn: foreign/private investment

words.definitionEn: a person or an organization that invests money in something
words.exampleEn: small investors (= private people)

words.definitionEn: to separate somebody/something physically or socially from other people or things
words.exampleEn: isolate somebody/yourself/something, Patients with the disease should be isolated.

words.definitionEn: far away from any others
words.exampleEn: isolated rural areas

words.definitionEn: to make something known formally
words.exampleEn: issue something, They issued a joint statement denying the charges.

words.definitionEn: a prison
words.exampleEn: She spent a year in jail.

words.definitionEn: a way of dealing with somebody/something; a way of doing or thinking about something such as a problem or a task
words.exampleEn: She took the wrong approach in her dealings with them.

words.definitionEn: to put somebody in prison
words.exampleEn: be jailed (for something), He was jailed for life for murder.

words.definitionEn: a plane driven by jet engines
words.exampleEn: a jet aircraft/fighter/airliner

words.definitionEn: involving two or more people together
words.exampleEn: a joint account (= a bank account in the name of more than one person, for example shared by a couple)

words.definitionEn: to come near to somebody/something in distance or time
words.exampleEn: We could hear the train approaching.

words.definitionEn: a place where two bones are joined together in the body in a way that enables them to bend and move
words.exampleEn: inflammation of the knee joint

words.definitionEn: the work of collecting and writing news stories for newspapers, magazines, radio, television or online news sites; the news stories that are written
words.exampleEn: I'd like a career in journalism.

words.definitionEn: a feeling of great happiness
words.exampleEn: Her books have brought joy to millions.

words.definitionEn: suitable, acceptable or correct for the particular circumstances
words.exampleEn: an appropriate response/measure/method

words.definitionEn: the ability to make sensible decisions after carefully considering the best thing to do
words.exampleEn: good/poor/sound judgement

words.definitionEn: having a low rank in an organization or a profession
words.exampleEn: a junior partner/minister/officer

words.definitionEn: in a way that is suitable, acceptable or correct for the particular circumstances
words.exampleEn: The government has been accused of not responding appropriately to the needs of the homeless.

words.definitionEn: a group of members of the public who listen to the facts of a case in a court and decide whether or not somebody is guilty of a crime, or whether a claim has been proved
words.exampleEn: members of the jury

words.definitionEn: the fair treatment of people
words.exampleEn: She spent her life fighting for social justice and equality for women.

words.definitionEn: to show that somebody/something is right or reasonable
words.exampleEn: justify doing something, How can they justify paying such huge salaries?

words.definitionEn: the feeling that somebody/something is good or acceptable; a positive opinion of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She desperately wanted to win her father's approval.

words.definitionEn: to think that somebody/something is good, acceptable or suitable
words.exampleEn: I told my mother I wanted to leave school but she didn't approve.

words.definitionEn: a set of tools or equipment that you use for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: a first-aid kit

words.definitionEn: work, especially physical work
words.exampleEn: manual labour (= work using your hands)

words.definitionEn: a piece of equipment for climbing up and down a wall, the side of a building, etc., consisting of two lengths of wood or metal that are joined together by steps or rungs
words.exampleEn: to climb up/fall off a ladder

words.definitionEn: an act of bringing an aircraft or a spacecraft down to the ground after a journey
words.exampleEn: a perfect/smooth/safe landing

words.definitionEn: everything you can see when you look across a large area of land, especially in the country
words.exampleEn: the woods and fields that are typical features of the English landscape

words.definitionEn: a narrow road in the country
words.exampleEn: winding country lanes

words.definitionEn: to a great extent; mostly or mainly
words.exampleEn: The manager was largely responsible for the team’s victory.

words.definitionEn: recently; in the recent past
words.exampleEn: Have you seen her lately?

words.definitionEn: the most recent or the newest thing or piece of news
words.exampleEn: the latest (in something), This is the latest in robot technology.

words.definitionEn: the action of launching something
words.exampleEn: a missile/rocket launch

words.definitionEn: to start an activity, especially an organized one
words.exampleEn: The government recently launched a national road safety campaign.

words.definitionEn: the state or position of being a leader
words.exampleEn: a leadership role/position

words.definitionEn: a printed sheet of paper or a few printed pages that are given free to advertise or give information about something
words.exampleEn: We picked up a few leaflets on local places of interest.

words.definitionEn: a group of sports teams who all play each other to earn points and find which team is best
words.exampleEn: Castleford have led the league for most of the season.

words.definitionEn: to bend or move from a straight position to a sloping position
words.exampleEn: I leaned back in my chair.

words.definitionEn: a period of time when you are allowed to be away from work for a holiday or for a special reason
words.exampleEn: to take a month’s paid/unpaid leave

words.definitionEn: a story from ancient times about people and events, that may or may not be true; this type of story
words.exampleEn: The film is based on the legend of Robin Hood.

words.definitionEn: a curved piece of glass or plastic that makes things look larger, smaller or clearer when you look through it
words.exampleEn: a pair of glasses with tinted lenses

words.definitionEn: to make something flat or smooth
words.exampleEn: level something out, The first coat of plaster levels out the surface of the wall.

words.definitionEn: an official document that shows that permission has been given to do, own or use something
words.exampleEn: Applicants must hold a valid driving licence.

words.definitionEn: to happen; to start to exist
words.exampleEn: An opportunity arose to work in the United States.

words.definitionEn: the length of time that somebody lives or that something lasts
words.exampleEn: a lifetime of experience

words.definitionEn: the arrangement or type of light in a place
words.exampleEn: electric/natural lighting

words.definitionEn: the same; in a similar way
words.exampleEn: He voted for the change and he expected his colleagues to do likewise.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of limiting or controlling somebody/something
words.exampleEn: They would resist any limitation of their powers.

words.definitionEn: not very great in amount or extent
words.exampleEn: We are doing our best with the limited resources available.

words.definitionEn: to cover the inside of something with a layer of another material, especially to keep it clean or make it stronger
words.exampleEn: Butter and line a 25 cm cake tin.

words.definitionEn: involving the use of weapons
words.exampleEn: an armed robbery

words.definitionEn: in a literal way
words.exampleEn: The word ‘planet’ literally means ‘wandering body’.

words.definitionEn: connected with literature
words.exampleEn: literary criticism/theory

words.definitionEn: weapons, especially as used by the army, navy, etc.
words.exampleEn: arms and ammunition

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring volume, equal to 1.76 British pints or 2.11 American pints
words.exampleEn: 3 litres of water

words.definitionEn: small pieces of rubbish such as paper, cans and bottles, that people have left lying in a public place
words.exampleEn: There will be fines for people who drop litter.

words.definitionEn: full of life and energy; active and enthusiastic
words.exampleEn: an intelligent and lively young woman

words.definitionEn: something that is being carried (usually in large amounts) by a person, vehicle, etc.
words.exampleEn: The trucks waited at the warehouse to pick up their loads.

words.definitionEn: to put a large quantity of things or people onto or into something
words.exampleEn: load something, We loaded the car in ten minutes.

words.definitionEn: money that an organization such as a bank lends and somebody borrows
words.exampleEn: to take out/repay a loan (= to borrow money/pay it back)

words.definitionEn: seeming natural, reasonable or sensible
words.exampleEn: It was a logical conclusion from the child's point of view.

words.definitionEn: a printed design or symbol that a company or an organization uses as its special sign
words.exampleEn: All over the world there are red and white paper cups bearing the company logo.

words.definitionEn: lasting or having an effect over a long period of time
words.exampleEn: Our long-term goal is to lower operating costs by 10 per cent.

words.definitionEn: over a long period of time
words.exampleEn: to benefit/affect somebody long-term

words.definitionEn: not securely fixed where it should be; able to become separated from something
words.exampleEn: a loose button/tooth

words.definitionEn: (in the UK) a man of high rank in the nobility (= people of high social class), or somebody who has been given the title ‘lord’ as an honour
words.exampleEn: She's married to a lord.

words.definitionEn: a way of raising money for a government, charity, etc. by selling tickets that have different numbers on them that people have chosen. Numbers are then chosen by chance and the people who have those numbers on their tickets win prizes.
words.exampleEn: the national/state lottery

words.definitionEn: a low level or point; a low figure
words.exampleEn: The temperature reached a record low in London last night.

words.definitionEn: to reduce something or to become less in value, quality, etc.
words.exampleEn: lower something, He lowered his voice to a whisper.

words.definitionEn: remaining constant in your support of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: a loyal friend/supporter

words.definitionEn: either of the two organs in the chest that you use for breathing
words.exampleEn: Her father died of lung cancer.

words.definitionEn: the words of a popular song
words.exampleEn: music and lyrics by Rodgers and Hart

words.definitionEn: extremely attractive and impressive; deserving praise
words.exampleEn: The Taj Mahal is a magnificent building.

words.definitionEn: to make something continue at the same level, standard, etc.
words.exampleEn: to maintain law and order/standards/a balance/control

words.definitionEn: the largest part of a group of people or things
words.exampleEn: majority (of somebody/something), The majority of people interviewed prefer TV to radio.

words.definitionEn: the name or type of a machine, piece of equipment, etc. that is made by a particular company
words.exampleEn: make of something, What make of car does he drive?

words.definitionEn: coloured substances used on the skin to make yourself look more attractive or to change your appearance
words.exampleEn: eye make-up

words.definitionEn: the act or process of making or producing something
words.exampleEn: strategic decision-making

words.definitionEn: a thin stick with a sharp point at one end, which is shot from a bow
words.exampleEn: a bow and arrow

words.definitionEn: to make goods in large quantities, using machines
words.exampleEn: manufactured goods

words.definitionEn: the business or industry of producing goods in large quantities in factories, etc.
words.exampleEn: Many jobs in manufacturing were lost during the recession.

words.definitionEn: to make a map of an area
words.exampleEn: an unexplored region that has not yet been mapped

words.definitionEn: a long running race of about 42 kilometres or 26 miles
words.exampleEn: the London marathon
